2605.00270.title: Are You the A-hole? A Fair, Multi-Perspective Ethical Reasoning Framework
2605.00270.paper_summary: Standard methods for aggregating natural language judgments, such as majority voting, often fail to produce logically consistent results when applied to high-conflict domains, treating differing opinions as noise. We propose a neuro-symbolic aggregation framework that formalizes conflict resolution through Weighted Maximum Satisfiability (MaxSAT). Our pipeline utilizes a language model to map unstructured natural language explanations into interpretable logical predicates and confidence weights. These components are then encoded as soft constraints within the Z3 solver, transforming the aggregation problem into an optimization task that seeks the maximum consistency across conflicting testimony. Using the Reddit r/AmItheAsshole forum as a case study in large-scale moral disagreement, our system generates logically coherent verdicts that diverge from popularity-based labels 62% of the time, corroborated by an 86% agreement rate with independent human evaluators. This study demonstrates the efficacy of coupling neural semantic extraction with formal solvers to enforce logical soundness and explainability in the aggregation of noisy human reasoning.
2605.00270.paper_summary_zh: 標準的自然語言判斷聚合方法，如多數投票，常常在高衝突領域中無法產生邏輯一致的結果，將不同的意見視為噪音。我們提出了一個神經符號聚合框架，通過加權最大滿足度（MaxSAT）來形式化衝突解決。我們的流程利用語言模型將非結構化的自然語言解釋映射為可解釋的邏輯謂詞和信心權重。這些組件然後被編碼為Z3求解器中的軟約束，將聚合問題轉化為一個優化任務，尋求在衝突證詞中達到最大一致性。以Reddit r/AmItheAsshole論壇作為大規模道德分歧的案例研究，我們的系統生成的邏輯一致的判決與基於人氣的標籤有62%的偏差，並且與獨立人類評估者的協議率達到86%。這項研究展示了將神經語義提取與正式求解器結合的有效性，以在嘈雜的人類推理聚合中強化邏輯健全性和可解釋性。
2605.00270.author: Sheza Munir et.al.
2605.00270.authors: Sheza Munir, Ahanaf Rodoshi, Sumin Lee, Feiran Chang, Xujie Si, Syed Ishtiaque Ahmed
